**Student Name:**	**Weight:**	30%

**Student ID:**	**Marks:**	/80

# **Project Development**

## **Introduction**

Project development involves creating a probabilistic model that accurately represents the connection between independent and dependent variables. For this phase, you will construct multiple machine learning models based on your problem statement. This will include using the Cross-Industry Standard Process for Data Mining (CRISP-DM) methodology which provides a structured approach for developing machine learning models.

The main goal of this phase is to create an efficient prediction model that automates the task and provides valuable insights based on a given dataset.

![][image1]

**Figure 1: The CRISP-DM Methodology**

© 2012, Kenneth Jensen, [https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:CRISP-DM\_Process\_Diagram.png](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:CRISP-DM_Process_Diagram.png)   
([CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/deed.en))

**Instructions**

**Note:**	The due date for this phase is located in the course calendar (found in the *Course Information* section in D2L). Files submitted after the due date will receive a mark of zero (0). 

1. Review the marking criteria section below.

2. Attend the lecture on the correct use of CRISP-DM.

3. Complete the reading activities. 

4. Part A – Stages of the Machine Learning Project

According to CRISP-DM, the life cycle of any machine learning project involves 6 stages, from business understanding to model deployment. The following tasks need to be completed to develop an end-to-end machine learning project:

**Notes:**	

* Complete these tasks in Jupyter notebook.

* Every code snippet in Jupyter notebook must be well-documented in the form of comments, paragraphs and relevant headings.

  1. Business Understanding 

  For this step, you will apply the business understanding that you developed in the previous phases (proposal writing, project planning and problem statement). 

     1. Describe a real-world problem/scenario related to a specific industry as defined in the project proposal writing phase.

     2. Explain the business context, objectives, and the impact of solving this problem using machine learning.

  2. Data Collection and Data Understanding

     1. Collect the data from data sources specified in the design phase.

     2. Analyze (perform EDA) the dataset using both visual and non-visual representation (data types, descriptive statistics, missing values, data distribution, outliers, etc.).

     3. Define the dependent variable and independent variables.

  3. Data Preparation

     1. Wrangle the data (handle missing values, anomalies, etc.).

     2. Perform feature engineering (e.g., categorical to numeric conversions, normalizations, standardization, etc.).

     3. Analyze (perform EDA) the dataset again after the cleaning and feature engineering operations.

     4. Split the data into training and test set.

  4. Model Building

     1. Select the appropriate machine learning algorithms (e.g., classification, regression, clustering, NLP, CV).

     2. Use the training dataset to train the model.

  5. Model Evaluation

     1. Use the test dataset to perform model evaluation.

     2. Use appropriate evaluation criteria to check the performance of the model (e.g., Accuracy, RMSE, etc.).

     3. Perform hyperparameter tuning (if required) to improve performance.

  6. Model Deployment and Monitoring (optional)

     1. Deploy the model as a web app.

     2. Monitor the model performance in production.

5. Part B – Develop a Progress Report

   1. Review the *Progress Report Template* in Brightspace.

   2. Use the provided template to divide and track project work.

6. Only one copy needs to be submitted per team. Your submission should include:

   1. Detailed answers to the questions on Jupyter notebook (.ipynb)

   2. Pickle file (by converting the .ipynb file into a pickle file) (optional)

   3. A URL of the deployed web app on the cloud (optional)

   4. A completed progress report (.docx or .pdf)

   5. The names of all team members listed at the beginning of each document 

## **Marking Criteria**

| Criteria | Needs Improvement | Good | Excellent | Marks |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | :---: |
| **Business Understanding** | Business understanding is missing from the Jupyter notebook. (0 marks) | Some of the components of business understanding are covered. (5 marks) | All the mentioned components under the business understanding stage are properly covered and relevant to the project. (10 marks)  | **/10** |
| **Data Understanding** | Only non-visual EDA is performed.  (5 marks) | Some of the data analysis is complete. (10 marks) | In-depth EDA (univariant, bi-variant, and multivariant), both visual and non-visual analysis, is performed and tells a clear story of historical data. (20 marks) | **/20** |
| **Data Preparation** | Some of the important aspects of data cleaning are missing. (7 marks) | Data is prepared and clean is complete but feature engineering is incomplete, leading to a biased result. (10 marks) | Relevant data cleaning and feature engineering are performed. (20 marks)  | **/20** |
| **Model Building** | Model training is missing or mostly incomplete. (2 marks) | Model training has been added but some areas are incomplete. Only 1 or 2 algorithms are used to train the model. (6 marks) | All model training components are complete, clear and concise. Most of the relevant machine learning algorithms are used to train the model. (10 marks) | **/10** |
| **Model Evaluation** | Model evaluation is missing. (0 marks) | Model evaluation is complete but important details are missing. Relevant metrics are not used to check the performance of the model. (6 marks) | Model evaluation is complete and explanation comments are provided. (10 marks) | **/10** |
| **Progress Report** | The progress report is missing or incomplete. (2 marks) | The progress report lacks detail and/or tasks are not evenly distributed. (6 marks) | The progress report is complete and work has been evenly distributed. (10 marks) | **/10** |
| **Total** |  |  |  | **/80** |
